key_points: A right-angled triangle has one angle equal to 90°
The side opposite the right angle is called the hypotenuse
The Baudhāyana-Pythagoras theorem relates the three sides of a right triangle
The square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides
The diagonal of a square forms two right triangles
exam_tips: Use squares of numbers when applying the theorem
Check if a triangle is right-angled by verifying a² + b² = c²
